title: podman 4 on fedora 35 + btrfs fails with "setxattr ...: operation not supported
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am trying to run podman 4 (podman-3.0.3-1.fc35.x86_64) on Fedora 35.
I've followed [these instructions](https://podman.io/blogs/2022/03/06/why_no_podman4_f35.html) and I have successfully run `podman system reset --force`.

Attempting to start a new container fails like this:

```
$ podman run -it --rm docker.io/alpine:latest sh
Error: error creating container storage: error creating read-write layer with ID "a06756ea9345d4a21131b229c64d2d886ea1cecf1ae15e48921363b60be04cff": setxattr /home/lars/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/a06756ea9345d4a21131b229c64d2d886ea1cecf1ae15e48921363b60be04cff/etc/mtab: operation not supported
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I was previously running podman 3 on this system (successfully).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Fri Apr  1 14:21:14 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.0.3-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


This is the most recent version of podman available via the F35 copr.

comments.body: This happens both with root and rootless podman.

Is there a specific reason you use the btrfs driver instead of overlayfs?

I think this might be related https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13152
@larsks Did you deliberately select the btrfs driver?
This is the SELinux setting labels on symbolic links issues, which will be fixed in podman 4.1. And is fixed in main branch now.
Is there an easy workaround in the meantime?

Or just downgrade to podman 3.x, which isn't broken, and wait for the fix mentioned in [dan's earlier comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13820#issuecomment-1095458080) to hit the package repositories.
